All right? Take care. There's a level of beauty with the straight line that once you really get it, it's like, whoa, it's like, it's like, it's almost like when Neo has that moment in the matrix, what almost suddenly realizes that he doesn't have to dodge bolts anymore. He controls the bull. It's really like, it's just profound as that. It really is. I've seen people go from not being able to close a door to like becoming world-class clothes. It's like a turning point where like, oh my God, I truly understand what is going on when I open up my mouth. And every moment I'm speaking, listening, I know exactly what I should be doing at that moment. I know what's happening in that moment. And once you get to that point, it's just like, it's so empowering, both for closing and teaching others to do the same, for motivating yourself, for negotiating. It's just all aspects of life. Communicating with a partner, with your children, people you love, right? You can't get there without understanding the why, the psychology behind this. So let's start talking about some of this psychology right now. For example, the first lesson of unconscious communication starts with this whole notion about, I'm joking around about judging books by their covers and how my parents do it and so do why and so do all of us. In those first few seconds of a conversation, you are being judged. That's it. You must accept this fact. In the first three or four seconds or quite the whole university, it's seven seconds, you're being judged, you're being ripped apart, put back together based on how you're perceived. Each piece of you is judged, compartmentalized. This is a fact of life, guys. And guys, I mean, guys, girls, and all you z's out there. I hate that stuff, right? Anyway, the point is, is that you're being judged by how you look, by how you dress, by the tone of your voice, by your body movements, by every word that comes out of your mouth, the tonality you apply to it, you're being judged. It happens like that in a few seconds. I gave an example, I think last week I said about, I'm a massive respect for the John Maxwell team. They're probably listening right now, right? Most, they're more integrity than any sales force out there, right? They were able to have a 20-plus percent increase in their closing rate, just by focusing on the first four seconds. They started using the streetlight. The first thing is, okay, let's all focus on that. People were skeptical and bam, 20% increase because of the first four seconds. Why? Because they're not going to be able to do that. They're going to be able to do that for the first four seconds. Why? Why is it, how could that be? Just think about that. Let's say you're making, let's say you're making to a hundred grand a year right now, a hundred thousand a year, and you increase your closing rate by 20%. What does that mean to you dollar-wise? It means more than making 120,000. You're not just going to make it extra 20, because each person you close, remember, makes more money if you're over the life cost of what you get, referrals. Think about the power of just mastering these first four seconds, making it a commitment to yourself to not leave these first four seconds up to chance, whether you're in person or over the phone.
